desktop: Open each flow-variant preview map from both guide-seat and river-eye review cameras.
Record scalability preset, resolution, frame-time distribution, game/render/GPU thread costs, draw calls/primitives, and GPU memory.
Attach notes proving flow-specific holes, laterals, boils, wave trains, wet banks, swimmer drift, and rescue targets remain readable.
vr: Run each variant in the OpenXR/VR profile or target HMD simulator with comfort settings recorded.
Record target render resolution, refresh rate, frame-time distribution, dropped/reprojected frames, thread/GPU costs, and GPU memory.
Attach comfort, motion readability, hazard/rescue readability, and guide-seat visibility notes per flow state.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: South Fork low-runnable review band: expose more rocks and shallows, tighten tongues, reduce foam, and preserve rescue/hazard readability before guide approval.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: South Fork median summer-commercial review band with readable tongues, wet rocks, eddy lines, and moderate wave trains.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: South Fork high-water review band: raise wet banks, strengthen laterals and wave trains, and keep boulder hazards visible instead of visually washing them away.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: Colorado low-release planning band with more exposed bars and sharper ferry setup while preserving big-water scale and swimmer visibility.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: Colorado moderate-release planning band with longer tongues, lateral waves, broad current streaks, and default oar-rig sightline readability.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: Colorado high-release review band with faster big-water read, stronger wave trains, eddy fences, and inspectable shore/rescue cues.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: Pacuare clear-season low planning band with exposed rocks, tighter tongues, clearer shallow-water cues, and reduced bank-pressure visuals.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: Pacuare rainfed runnable planning band with pushy tongues, active wave trains, wet banks, and vegetation pressure after rain.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: Pacuare rainy-season high planning band with faster reaction windows, larger laterals, fewer visible eddies, and stronger swimmer drift cues.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

flow_context.flow_visual_note: Pacuare flash-response review-only band blocked from playable use until hydrology and guide review define safe visual and gameplay boundaries.

variants.current_decision: Static map and capture inventory is recorded for this flow variant, but measured desktop and VR profile evidence is still required before the variant can be treated as playable or lifelike.

current_decision: This artifact extends performance review coverage from the default river captures to every flow variant. It is still an evidence scaffold only; real Unreal profiling captures must be attached before any flow band is marked production-playable or lifelike-approved.
